author: cferrugem
description: Search your vault with a fast, worker-powered index. Typo-tolerant fuzzy matching, in-file search, and optional PDF, image, and Office document indexing. Inspired by Omnisearch. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: cferrugem
manifest.description: Search your vault with a fast, worker-powered index. Typo-tolerant fuzzy matching, in-file search, and optional PDF, image, and Office document indexing. Inspired by Omnisearch.
manifest.name: CFR Find

name: CFR Find

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.9

New setting: **Reset settings to defaults** (under Cache). Restores every CFR Find option to its default value with a two-click confirmation — the button asks you to click again within 4 seconds, so an accidental tap does nothing. If an indexing-related option changes as a result, the index updates automatically.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.8

Opening a search result no longer switches your note out of reading view. The jump now respects whichever mode the note is in:

- **Reading view**: the note scrolls to the match's line (same mechanism internal links use) — your view mode is left untouched. Reading view has no text selection, so there is no highlight there by design.
- **Editing view (source/live preview)**: unchanged — the matched word is selected and scrolled into view.

No changes to search behavior or the index.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.7

Mobile/UX polish based on real phone usage:

- **Excerpts no longer show raw frontmatter.** Matches inside the YAML block are ignored when picking the excerpt, so result cards show note content instead of `--- data: … revisar: false ---`.
- **Compact result cards.** Excerpts clamp to 3 lines (2 on mobile) with ellipsis; on mobile the search bar and cards use tighter spacing, and long file paths ellipsize to one line.

No changes to search behavior or the index.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.6

Fixes the last actionable finding from the community directory scorecard: the "Clear cache and rebuild" button used `setDestructive()`, an API newer than the declared minimum Obsidian version (1.5.7) — a potential crash on older installs. The button now gets its destructive styling via the `mod-warning` CSS class, which every Obsidian version understands.

No behavior changes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.5

Maintenance release addressing the community directory's automated review findings:

- Popout-window compatibility: `window.setTimeout`/`window.clearTimeout` on the main thread (the worker uses its own global scope), and Obsidian's `appendText` helper instead of `document.createTextNode` in excerpt rendering.
- IndexedDB promise rejections are now always `Error` instances; cache reads are properly typed.
- Removed deprecated APIs (`setDynamicTooltip`, `setWarning` → `setDestructive`), unused exports, unnecessary type assertions, and a partially-supported CSS property.
- Replaced the `builtin-modules` package with Node's own `builtinModules`.
- Release assets now ship with GitHub artifact attestations (cryptographic provenance).
- README gained a privacy section: CFR Find makes no network requests; the only external link is the optional Buy Me a Coffee button in settings, opened only on click. Added a contributing guide.

No behavior changes to search itself.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.4

- While you're still typing, the word in progress now only prefix-expands from 2 characters — a single letter no longer fans out to a huge slice of the index on every keystroke.
- The duplicated jump-to-match logic from both modals now lives in one shared helper (same behavior: switches reading view to source mode and retries once the editor settles).
- Added regression tests locking in typed-word phrase anchoring: "recuperação de ar" anchors the excerpt and jump on "Recuperação de arquivos", while middle stopwords stay exact so "de" can never anchor on "desktop".

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Release v1.0.3

### What's new:
- **Highlight Bug Fix:** Fixed short terms (like "de") highlighting entire expanded words (like "dentro", "dele").
- **Real-time Prefix Matching:** The last query term is now always matched by prefix in real-time, even if it has only 1 character.
- **Resilient Jump-to-Match:** Fixed notes opening at the top instead of scrolling to the matched line. Swaps to edit mode if needed to show selection.
- **Modernized UI Design:** Premium visual identity with backdrop blur (Glassmorphism), smooth GPU-accelerated transition animations, and mobile-friendly performance tweaks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.2

- **Fixed misaligned highlights** (marks starting mid-word): the excerpt renderer collapsed newline runs into one space, shifting every highlight after them — especially visible on files with Windows (CRLF) line endings. Replacement is now length-preserving and content is normalized before offsets are computed.
- **Excerpts now show the note's best passage**: if your query occurs as a (near-)phrase — "recuperação de arquivo" matches "Recuperação de arquivos" — the excerpt centers on it; otherwise it picks the window containing the most distinct query terms instead of the first stray token.
- **Enter jumps to that same best passage**, not the first token in the file.
- **In-file search is now ranked**: one row per line, lines containing the query as a near-phrase first, then lines matching more distinct terms; filler words like "de" no longer flood the list or the highlights.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.1

- **Smarter ranking with partial-match fallback**: precise (all-terms) matches rank first; when there is room left, notes matching only some terms appear below them, scored by how many terms they matched. Searches like "recuperação de arquivos" now surface notes that phrase it differently.
- **Self-healing index**: every time you open the search, the vault is re-diffed (path + modification time) against the index, catching anything a missed event or a sync/git pull left stale. The first search waits for this check, so results are never computed against an outdated index.
- **Better Tab flow**: in vault search, Tab drills into the *selected* result (without opening it); Enter inside a note opens it right at the chosen match; Tab/Shift+Tab returns to vault search keeping your query.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## CFR Find 1.0.0 — initial release

Fast, worker-powered full-text search for Obsidian. Heavily inspired by [Omnisearch](https://github.com/scambier/obsidian-omnisearch) by Simon Cambier (GPL-3).

### Features
- Vault-wide ranked search running entirely in a Web Worker — the Obsidian UI never freezes, even while indexing
- In-file search with Tab handoff between the two modes
- Typo-tolerant fuzzy matching with configurable tolerance, minimum fuzzy word length, and prefix threshold
- Query syntax: "exact phrases", -exclusions, ext:/path: filters
- Diacritics-insensitive and camelCase-aware matching
- Persistent index cache (IndexedDB) — restarts only re-index changed files
- Optional PDF / image (OCR) / Office indexing via the [Text Extractor](https://github.com/scambier/obsidian-text-extractor) plugin (disabled by default; enabling shows a slowdown warning)
- Works on desktop and mobile

### Credits
- [Omnisearch](https://github.com/scambier/obsidian-omnisearch) by Simon Cambier — inspiration
- [MiniSearch](https://github.com/lucaong/minisearch) by Luca Ongaro — search engine library
- [Text Extractor](https://github.com/scambier/obsidian-text-extractor) by Simon Cambier — optional PDF/image/Office extraction
